title: Cotswolds: Naunton, Guiting Power & Bourton-on-the-Water

main_route.excerpt: We left at 5.40 + got to Naunton at [?] 7.10. We have camped at the back of a pub
We drove to Bourton-on-the-Water & couldn't find the hotel we stayed in. We all enjoyed the model village.

stops.excerpt: We left about 9.30. to walk to Power Guiting, along narrow roads, past horse farms, over foot bridges. We reached the [illegible] village + had trouble finding a cup of coffee.

summary: A weekend camping trip to the Cotswolds in late September 1993, with the family and German au pair Chris Goerdt camping behind a pub at Naunton. Day activities included a walk to Guiting Power on Saturday and a visit to the model village at Bourton-on-the-Water on the drive home Sunday.
extraction_notes: All three days clearly dated. The trip is entirely within England with no crossings. Guiting Power is recorded as 'Power Guiting' in the diary — normalized to correct order. Bourton-on-the-Water visited on Sunday 26th appears to be on the return journey to Birmingham rather than an excursion back to Naunton, so treated as a main_stop. The mention of 'the hotel we stayed in' at Bourton suggests a previous family visit there. Page A_0116_L has low legibility (0.55) with a glued-in photo but no additional route information. Tinder the dog is not mentioned in these pages but may have attended.
